description:
PREMISE (measured)
The quoted-source fingerprint (src/compiler2/source.rs) is eager dead weight. QuotedSourceCarrier::new fingerprints EVERY parsed code on every IndexCode: fingerprint_value walks the whole quoted graph building a canonical String per node, concatenated upward (O(n*depth)), then FNV-hashes it and RETAINS the canonical string. In production only semantic.digest is read; canonical is read only by tests. On a fresh compile the digest is never compared, so the entire pass is wasted. Measured ~27% of IndexCode on quicksort, and it scales worse with source size/depth.

INSIGHT
Computing a content hash costs a full O(n) pass. A semantic deep-compare costs the SAME O(n) when equal but FAST-FAILS at the first difference (the common 'it changed' case), and is paid LAZILY only when a definition is actually replaced -- never on first index. So the fingerprint is strictly dominated.

SEAM (already exists)
identity.rs:666 replace_if_changed<T: SameState> is the universal revision gate: same_state==true -> keep revision; else bump. code.rs:index does the same for code. SameState IS the per-fact-kind comparable the design wants. Today its quoted-source arms reach for precomputed identity:
  - same_code_state (code.rs:104): left.quoted.semantic.digest == right...
  - ModuleSource::same_source (identity.rs:709): self.source.semantic.digest == other...
  - FunctionState/FunctionSourceState (identity.rs:722,739): left.source.key() == right.source.key()  <-- reference identity (heap_id+root); every re-parse makes a fresh heap so key() ALWAYS differs => functions OVER-INVALIDATE on any resubmission even when byte-identical. Latent precision bug.

PLAN
1. Add QuotedSourceRoot::semantically_eq(&self, &other) -> bool: walk both quoted graphs in lockstep across their two Processes, fast-fail at first difference. Mirror fingerprint_value's traversal but compare instead of serialize. Cross-heap rules: atoms by rendered NAME (atom ids differ per heap), structs by schema NAME+arity then fields in order, lists by spine in lockstep (length must match), maps by included entries, bitstrings by bytes+bitlen. SKIP exactly META_SPAN_KEY and META_NAMESPACE_ID_KEY (the current Semantic policy) -- this preserves today's invalidation semantics by construction.
2. Rewire all four SameState arms to semantically_eq; delete the digest and key() compares.
3. DELETE the fingerprint: QuotedSourceFingerprint, the 'semantic' field on QuotedSourceCarrier, fingerprint_root/value/map/struct/bitstring, digest_fnv64, QuotedSourceFingerprintPolicy (only Semantic mattered), eager compute in QuotedSourceCarrier::new, mod.rs exports.
4. Tests: ~14 assertions on semantic.canonical in source_test.rs + frontdoor_test.rs. DECISION: keep a lazy on-demand render_semantic() debug helper (NOT stored/hashed/eager) so tests change minimally, OR rewrite them structurally. Default: lazy renderer, decoupled from identity.
5. Strip the TEMP(fz-gli-probe) index_code sub-step spans + TelemetryExt import in jobs/source.rs.
6. Docs: update any .agent doc describing source identity/fingerprinting.

RISK
Step 2 changes function-level invalidation from 'always bump' to 'bump iff semantically changed'. This is the intended improvement but alters incremental behavior -- needs targeted tests (resubmit identical fn -> revision stable; change a fn body -> revision bumps; reposition/whitespace -> stable; change span-only -> stable).

NOT IN SCOPE: modules/interface.rs fingerprint_digest(fingerprint_inputs) is a separate module-interface mechanism; leave it.

TDD: unit tests on semantically_eq (ignores span/ns, fast-fails, deep equality) before rewiring; revision-stability tests via the public submit/drive path.

DEP NOTE: lands after the uncommitted fz-gli codegen-telemetry change is committed and the temp probes are stripped.

comments.text:
DESIGN LOCKED (refined w/ architect):

Not 'prune', just SKIP — the quoted heap is immutable and there is ONE representation per parse. The module surface and each FunctionSource fact reference the SAME heap nodes by Rc (index_function -> note_function_source carries function.source, an Rc-bump clone). So no body-free copy is built; the comparables are just different TRAVERSALS over the shared immutable graph.

Each fact's comparable = one fast-fail walk parameterized by its HORIZON:
- Code/module identity (same_code_state, ModuleSource::same_source): 'deep-ish' — descend through the surface (clause signatures incl. params+guards, @spec, @doc, struct/protocol/import/alias shapes) and STOP at each clause's do: body subtree. Boundary = the existing clause head/body seam (the one lower_clause_body_fn / FunctionSource already use). A body-only edit => module/code revision STABLE.
- Function identity (FunctionSourceState/FunctionState): full to-the-leaves walk INCLUDING the body, fast-fail. Body edit => only that function's fact bumps. Replaces today's source.key() reference-identity (which over-invalidates every fn on any resubmission).

Resolved boundary question: 'head' = full clause signature (name/arity/flags + params + guards) + @spec + @doc. So a guard/pattern edit bumps module identity (dispatch-visible); only the do: expression is body. Confirmed by architect ('deep-ish').

Implementation reuses the existing body-boundary seam rather than inventing one. No fingerprint, no canonical, no digest anywhere.

title: Kill the quoted-source fingerprint: replace digest identity with a fast-fail semantic deep-compare on SameState
